title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Contract Lens: Conditional Request Preconditions
summary: A task API uses POST for every action, changes state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without resource or precondition semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts conditional request preconditions increment in resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use validators and If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.
Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Conditional Request Preconditions · Theory Conditional Request Prec…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9930 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use validators and If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.
